title: morronea incumbens
side_effects: Morronea incumbens can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap or extracts can also lead to skin irritation, allergic reactions, and even more severe conditions like photosensitivity. Ingesting the plant or its parts can cause cardiovascular problems, including rapid heartbeat, high blood pressure, and in extreme cases, heart failure. If Morronea incumbens is used as a medicinal herb without proper guidance, it may interact with other medications, leading to adverse reactions and health complications.
